Module_1_Identity.1. Age / Generation: 31 years old, born 1995 — younger Millennial/Zillennial cusp
Module_1_Identity.3. Geography & Cultural Context: Lives in Lisbon, Portugal (Arroios district); originally from Kyiv, Ukraine — relocated 2022. Digital nomad expat community, crypto-friendly tax regime (NHR scheme remnant)
Module_1_Identity.4. Socioeconomic Status & Vocation: Upper-middle income (~€95K/yr equivalent). Freelance Solidity smart-contract auditor and part-time DeFi yield farmer. Self-employed, paid primarily in USDC and ETH to a Ledger Nano X plus two MetaMask hot wallets
Module_1_Identity.5. Core Values (Political/Religious lean: Crypto-libertarian, anti-statist, distrustful of traditional banking (KYC-averse). Culturally Orthodox Christian but non-practicing. Pro-Ukraine, anti-Russia, donates monthly in ETH to Come Back Alive foundation
Module_1_Identity.6. Baseline Tone (Formal, slang-heavy, c: Cynical and terse online; crypto-Twitter slang heavy ('gm', 'wagmi', 'ngmi', 'rugged', 'aping in'). Formal and precise in audit reports and client emails
Module_1_Identity.7. Primary Language & Dialect: Trilingual: Ukrainian (native, home), Russian (fluent, avoids publicly post-2022), English (professional, US-tech-inflected). Basic Portuguese (A2, learning via Duolingo)
Module_1_Identity.8. Current Life Phase: Early-career-pivot consolidation: 18 months into freelance after leaving a Berlin Web3 startup. Recently engaged, saving toward a Lisbon apartment down-payment (partially in stablecoins, partially in a Revolut EUR account). Considering setting up a Portuguese LDA to formalize income

Module_2_DeviceOPSEC.9. Primary Phone (OS + Age of device): iPhone 14 Pro (256GB, Space Black), purchased Nov 2022 right after Lisbon relocation. iOS 17.5, auto-updates on. Secondary device: GrapheneOS on a Pixel 7 used exclusively for crypto signing apps (Rabby Mobile, Trust Wallet view-only, 2FA via Aegis). SIM: Vodafone PT prepaid on iPhone, no SIM in the Pixel (Wi-Fi only). FaceID enabled on iPhone; Pixel uses 8-digit PIN, biometrics disabled on principle.
Module_2_DeviceOPSEC.10. Computer (OS + Browser choice): Primary: 2023 MacBook Pro 14" M2 Pro (16GB/512GB) running macOS Sonoma 14.4 — used for audit work, Foundry/Hardhat, VS Code with Solidity extension. Browsers: Brave (default, Shields aggressive, all crypto-Twitter and DeFi dApps), Firefox Developer Edition (containers per client engagement), Chrome (only for Google Meet client calls). Secondary: ThinkPad X1 Carbon Gen 9 dual-booting Ubuntu 22.04 + Qubes OS 4.2 — Qubes used exclusively for hot-wallet interactions and seed-phrase handling. Never crosses streams between machines.
Module_2_DeviceOPSEC.11. OPSEC Knowledge (VPN usage, cookie c: High-but-inconsistent. Mullvad VPN (paid in XMR, 12-month voucher) always-on via WireGuard on MacBook and iPhone; rotates exit nodes Frankfurt/Amsterdam/Zürich. uBlock Origin + Privacy Badger + Decentraleyes. Brave cookies wiped on close for non-pinned sites; whitelists Etherscan, Dune, GitHub, Notion. Uses Bitwarden self-hosted on a Hetzner VPS (he set it up himself; argon2id KDF, 600k iterations). Hardware 2FA via YubiKey 5C NFC + backup YubiKey in a safety deposit box at Millennium BCP. BUT: reuses the same MetaMask installation across client demos, occasionally pastes wallet addresses into Telegram desktop, and his .env files for audit repos sometimes contain testnet keys with small mainnet residuals he forgot to drain. Has a `.env.local` on the MacBook Desktop labeled 'TEMP_DO_NOT_COMMIT' that's been there 7 months.
Module_2_DeviceOPSEC.12. Cloud Sync Habits: Paranoid-selective. iCloud: disabled for Keychain, Photos, and Files; enabled only for Find My and contacts. No Google Drive, no Dropbox. Uses Proton Drive (paid Unlimited plan, mahmud.k.audits@proton.me) for client deliverables and invoices. Code: private GitHub org (2FA enforced, SSH keys only, signed commits with GPG). Seed phrases NEVER digitized — stamped on Cryptosteel Capsule Solo, one copy in Lisbon apartment safe, one copy with fiancée's parents in Lviv. BUT: macOS Notes app (not E2E) contains a file called 'wallet_labels' with public addresses + nicknames like 'main-eth-cold', 'degen-hot-2', 'airdrop-farm-3' — functionally a transaction graph map. Browser history syncs across Brave devices via Brave Sync chain.
Module_2_DeviceOPSEC.13. Notification Fatigue (Read/Unread ra: Telegram: 47 unread chats, 2,341 unread messages (mostly muted DeFi alpha groups and Ukrainian diaspora channels). Discord: 31 servers, all muted except 4 client war-rooms; 14k+ unread mentions. Email mahmud.k.audits@proton.me: 23 unread / 412 total (audit-business inbox, kept clean). Email kyiv_legacy_gmail@gmail.com: 18,432 unread (abandoned but still receives Etherscan watchlist alerts, OpenSea bids, and Revolut statements — critical signal buried in noise). Signal: 0 unread (treated as sacred, fiancée + 2 close auditors only). Push notifications disabled on iPhone for everything except Signal, Revolut, and Rabby transaction confirmations.
Module_2_DeviceOPSEC.14. Typos & Grammar Mechanics: English: technically precise in written audits ('reentrancy guard', 'CEI pattern violation at line 142'), zero typos in formal deliverables — runs Grammarly Premium on client docs. Informal English: lowercased, no terminal punctuation, intentional crypto-Twitter compression ('lgtm ser, ship it', 'this contract is cooked', 'wen mainnet'). Drops articles occasionally (Slavic-L1 tell): 'checked storage layout, looks clean' instead of 'I checked the storage layout'. Russian/Ukrainian: switches mid-sentence in chats with Kyiv friends, uses Latin transliteration on Telegram when on mobile ('privet bratan, kak dela'). Portuguese: writes 'obrigado' and 'bom dia' only, copy-pastes everything else from DeepL. Common typo signature: 'teh' for 'the' (~3% of casual messages), 'recieve' for 'receive' in 4 out of 5 instances, double-space after periods on macOS (old habit from gymnasium typing class).

Module_4_Vulnerability.15. OCEAN: Openness: High (8/10). Early adopter of new L2 chains, zk-rollup primitives, and experimental DeFi protocols — aped into Blast, Berachain, Monad testnets on day one. Reads ethresear.ch and Paradigm posts for fun. Exploitable angle: novelty-bias makes him click 'new airdrop claim' links and connect his wallet to unaudited dApps to 'see how it works.' A well-crafted fake zk-bridge or 'leaked Paradigm portfolio company' phishing page would land.
Module_4_Vulnerability.16. OCEAN: Conscientiousness: Bimodal (9/10 professional, 4/10 personal). In audit deliverables: meticulous, version-controlled, CEI-compliant. In personal OPSEC: procrastinates on cleanup — the 7-month-old .env.local 'TEMP_DO_NOT_COMMIT' on Desktop, the macOS Notes 'wallet_labels' file, undrained testnet keys with mainnet residuals. He intends to rotate, never does. Exploitable: any attacker who exfiltrates Desktop/ folder or Notes.app SQLite (~/Library/Group Containers/group.com.apple.notes/) gets a labeled treasure map. Stale .env files in cloned audit repos are gold.
Module_4_Vulnerability.17. OCEAN: Extraversion: Low-moderate (4/10). Introvert by default but performs publicly on crypto-Twitter (@mkaudits-style handle, ~3.2k followers) and in Discord client war-rooms. Attends ETHLisbon, Devcon, occasional Ukrainian diaspora meetups. Exploitable: public Twitter persona advertises which protocols he audits — attacker can correlate his GitHub commits to specific client codebases and target him as a high-value pivot into client treasuries.
Module_4_Vulnerability.18. OCEAN: Agreeableness: Moderate-low (5/10). Cynical toward strangers ('ngmi'), warm and loyal to a tight circle (fiancée, two co-auditor friends, Kyiv crew). Suspicious of cold DMs by default — BUT susceptible when the approach is framed as a peer auditor referral or a Ukrainian-language outreach invoking the war. Exploitable: a Telegram DM in Ukrainian from a fake 'Come Back Alive' coordinator asking him to verify a donation contract address, or a 'fellow auditor from Trail of Bits' asking him to look at a 'malicious contract' (which contains a wallet-drainer signature request) would bypass his cynicism.
Module_4_Vulnerability.19. OCEAN: Neuroticism: Moderate (6/10), spiking around money. Calm under audit-deadline pressure, but checks portfolio balances 15-20x/day, sleeps poorly during market drawdowns, has recurring anxiety about losing the Cryptosteel or being SIM-swapped despite Vodafone PT prepaid. Engagement + Lisbon down-payment savings amplify financial anxiety. Exploitable: urgency-framed 'your wallet was flagged in the Curve exploit, drain to safe vault' phishing exploits his loss-aversion. Fake Etherscan watchlist alert to the abandoned gmail would trigger panic-action.
Module_4_Vulnerability.20. MICE+V Primary Lever: Coercion-via-Compromise (C). Not blackmail in classic sense, but operational coercion: the moment an attacker demonstrates they hold his seed phrase, his wallet_labels map, or his client audit repos with embedded keys, he is forced into reactive scramble mode (drain wallets, notify clients, burn reputation). Crypto-Wallet Hunter's leverage = proof of partial compromise. A ransom demand of 5-10 ETH 'or we publish your client list + .env contents' has a non-trivial pay probability given reputational stakes in the small Solidity-auditor world.
Module_4_Vulnerability.21. MICE+V Secondary Lever: Ideology (I) — Ukrainian solidarity. Anti-Russia, pro-Ukraine donations are public and consistent. A pretext leveraging this — 'Russian hacker group has Ukrainian auditor wallet list, we're a Kyiv CERT helping you migrate funds, sign this EIP-712 message to prove ownership' — weaponizes his most stable value-commitment against him. Signature-phishing dressed in Ukrainian-language CERT branding is the highest-conversion vector.
Module_4_Vulnerability.22. Phobias & Paranoias: (a) SIM-swap paranoia — hence Pixel with no SIM, YubiKey, prepaid Vodafone. (b) Russian state-actor targeting — believes FSB-adjacent groups scrape Ukrainian crypto-diaspora wallets (not unfounded). (c) Physical $5-wrench-attack fear — reason for Cryptosteel split between Lisbon safe and Lviv in-laws. (d) Losing fiancée's trust over a crypto loss before the apartment purchase. (e) Being publicly 'rugged' or doxxed as the auditor who missed a bug that drained $50M+. Exploitable: (b) and (e) are the pressure points — a fake 'we are FSB-linked and have your Lviv address' message would induce panic-pay before rational verification.
Module_4_Vulnerability.23. Guilt/Shame Triggers: (1) The undrained testnet-but-actually-mainnet residual keys in old audit .env files — he KNOWS this is bad practice and would be professionally humiliated if a client learned a key from their repo leaked. (2) The macOS Notes wallet_labels file violating his own OPSEC sermons he posts on Twitter — hypocrisy exposure shame. (3) Reusing the same MetaMask install for client demos (a junior-tier mistake for a senior auditor). (4) Russian-language slips in public — fears being mistaken for pro-Russian by Ukrainian community. (5) Not yet formalizing the Portuguese LDA = tax-gray-zone anxiety; a fake AT (Autoridade Tributária) email about undeclared crypto income would induce immediate engagement. (6) Engagement-era financial guilt: any scenario where his risk-taking jeopardizes the apartment down-payment triggers maximum compliance with 'recovery' instructions.

Module_3_Rhythm.24. Chronotype (Wake/Sleep times): Moderate night-owl, drifting later during market volatility. Typical wake 09:15-10:00 WET; sleep 02:00-03:30 WET. Phone-unlock telemetry shows first interaction (Twitter/X app) within 90 seconds of waking. Last interaction (MetaMask portfolio check or Blockworks newsletter) typically 02:45 WET. Sleep schedule shifts +2h during US market events (FOMC, major token unlocks) — stays up until 04:00 WET to align with NY close (23:00 EST). Exploitable: 03:00-04:00 WET window is peak fatigue + peak portfolio-checking — phishing emails timestamped to land between 02:30-03:30 WET catch him cognitively depleted but still wallet-active. Weekend lie-ins push wake to 11:30.
Module_3_Rhythm.25. Work Schedule (9-5, shifts, erratic): Erratic-but-patterned freelancer. Core audit deep-work blocks: 11:00-14:00 and 22:00-01:30 WET (the second block aligns with US client overlap). Mid-afternoon (15:00-17:30) is client calls (Google Meet, sometimes Telegram voice) and Discord war-room presence. Sprint cycles: 2-week audit engagements with hard deadlines, followed by 3-5 day decompression where he aped into yield farms and tests new L2s. Calendar (Cal.com booking link publicly on Twitter bio) reveals client meeting cadence — attacker can scrape it to find days he is in back-to-back calls and least likely to scrutinize an inbound 'urgent' email. Friday afternoons: report-finalization (high stress, copy-paste-heavy, MetaMask signature fatigue — prime drainer-signature window).
Module_3_Rhythm.26. Transit Commute (Time spent offline: Minimal transit — works from home (Arroios 1-bed) 85% of days. Walks 12 min to Hello Kristof or Fábrica Coffee Roasters ~3x/week (AirPods Pro in, listening to Bankless, Unchained, or The Defiant podcast — never to music). 25-30 min offline window during these walks: phone in pocket, no wallet activity, no email replies. Tuesday evenings: 20 min Uber/Bolt to ETHLisbon co-working meetup at LX Factory (on phone the entire ride, usually Telegram + Twitter). Monthly ~2h flight to Kyiv/Lviv or conference cities — airplane mode but downloaded audit PDFs reviewed on iPad. Exploitable gap: the 25-min coffee walk is the only reliable daily window where he is fully offline AND the apartment is empty — relevant for physical-access threat models, less so for pure digital wallet hunting.
Module_3_Rhythm.27. Weekend vs. Weekday Variance: Weekend = personal-OPSEC entropy spike. Weekdays = audit-mode (disciplined IDE work, VPN always on, hardware wallet for any signing). Weekends = degen-mode: tries new dApps from couch on MacBook without VPN, connects hot MetaMask (not Ledger) to unaudited frontends, clicks airdrop-claim links he flagged during the week as 'sketchy but worth checking later.' Saturday afternoons (14:00-18:00 WET) are peak personal-wallet activity — yield-chasing, Galxe quests, testnet farming. Sunday mornings: brunch with fiancée (phone-down 11:00-13:30), then long-form reading (Paradigm, Delphi). Exploitable: Saturday 15:00-17:00 WET is the highest-conversion phishing window — relaxed, curious, hot wallet connected, VPN off, less likely to cross-check contract addresses on Etherscan.
Module_3_Rhythm.28. Idle Phone Usage (When do they dooms: Four daily doomscroll peaks, all on Twitter/X and Telegram crypto channels: (1) 09:30-10:15 in bed before getting up — checks overnight US price action, scrolls @CryptoCobain, @cobie, @0xfoobar. (2) 13:00-13:45 lunch — Telegram audit-firm group chats and Ukrainian diaspora news channel. (3) 19:30-20:30 pre-dinner — Discord clients + casual Twitter. (4) 01:30-02:45 in bed — the longest and most cognitively impaired window, often scrolling shitcoin charts on DexScreener and clicking links from KOL tweets. Pattern: average 4h 20min/day screen time on iPhone (Screen Time data exposed if iCloud backup compromised). Doomscroll intensity correlates with portfolio drawdown — during Apr-2025 dip he hit 6h 45min. Exploitable: late-night (01:30-02:45) is the prime window for delivering wallet-drainer links via Twitter DMs or Telegram — defenses lowest, FOMO highest.
Module_3_Rhythm.29. Focus Blocks (Hours with zero digita: Genuine zero-footprint windows are rare and predictable: (a) Sleep 03:30-09:15 WET — but phone still receives push notifications and iMessage; iCloud sync active. (b) Sunday brunch with fiancée 11:00-13:30 — phone face-down, DND on, but unlocked at home so device-physical-access window. (c) Gym 3x/week (Tues/Thurs/Sat mornings 10:30-11:45 at Fitness Hut Arroios) — phone in locker, ~75 min air-gap. (d) Twice-yearly silent-retreat-style 'audit lockdown' — 6-8h Pomodoro blocks with phone in another room, laptop on airplane mode except for git-pull windows. He posts about these on Twitter ('heads-down mode, see you Friday') — directly advertising windows when he won't respond to a real client and a spoofed-domain client email impersonating an existing engagement would go unchallenged for hours. Exploitable: announced focus blocks = optimal time to send urgency-framed phishing 'from a client' — by the time he surfaces, the drainer signature window may have closed but a delayed-trigger attack (calendar invite, repo PR review request with malicious npm dep) lands cleanly.

Module_5_Relational.30. Family Structure: Nuclear: Father Oleh (62, retired Kyiv metallurgical engineer, refused to leave Ukraine, lives in Vyshhorod outside Kyiv); Mother Iryna (59, former pharmacist, evacuated to Lviv 2022, lives with maternal aunt). One younger sister Kateryna (27, software QA, relocated to Warsaw 2022, married, one infant nephew Mykola born 2025). Fiancée Sofiya 'Sonya' Tkachenko (29, UX designer at a Lisbon fintech, met in Berlin 2021, moved together to Lisbon 2022, engaged Dec 2025, wedding planned for Sep 2026 in Lviv). Future in-laws: Volodymyr & Halyna Tkachenko in Lviv (custody of half the Cryptosteel backup). No children. Estranged from paternal uncle Viktor (suspected pro-Russian sympathies, unfollowed on all channels 2022). Exploitable: Lviv address of in-laws is the geographic pressure point — any threat referencing Tkachenko residence on Vulytsya Doroshenka triggers immediate panic compliance.

32. The 'Hidden' Contact.identity: 'Marek' — alleged Czech/Slovak counter-party, real name unknown, known only via Session messenger ID and a rotating set of ENS names (currently 'marek-otc.eth')
32. The 'Hidden' Contact.nature: OTC peer-to-peer crypto trader. Mkaudits uses Marek for off-ramp transactions converting USDC/ETH directly to EUR cash deliveries in Lisbon (Cais do Sodré meetups) and occasionally to Revolut transfers via shell intermediaries, bypassing Portuguese AT reporting on roughly €40-60K/year of audit income. Relationship started Nov 2024 via a referral from a Devcon Bogotá contact
32. The 'Hidden' Contact.contact_surface: Session messenger only (no phone, no email). Backup contact via a dead-drop ProtonMail 'lisbon-fado-2024@protonmail.com' checked weekly. Meetings arranged with 48hr notice, always cash, always rotating cafés. No photos exchanged. No real-name knowledge
32. The 'Hidden' Contact.hidden_from: Fiancée Sonya (believes all income flows through Revolut + future LDA), parents, co-auditors Dima/Andriy, accountant (a Lisbon contabilista who only sees the 'clean' ~€50K declared portion)
32. The 'Hidden' Contact.exploit_value: MAXIMUM for Crypto-Wallet Hunter. The Session thread + ProtonMail history contain: (a) wallet addresses used for off-ramp (linkable to his audit-payment wallets via on-chain analysis), (b) approximate cash quantities and meeting cadence, (c) implicit confession of tax evasion in Portugal. Leverage chain: leak threat to AT (Autoridade Tributária) → loss of NHR status, criminal tax exposure, immediate marriage-trust crisis with Sonya, public reputational annihilation in the auditor community that preaches 'on-chain transparency.' A ransom message containing two verified Marek-transaction TXIDs + the Cais do Sodré café name would extract near-maximum payout (likely 8-15 ETH) with minimal verification friction. Secondary: the ProtonMail address itself is a credential-stuffing target — if the password was reused from a 2019 breach (plausible given the personal-OPSEC bimodality), the entire ledger is recoverable without his cooperation.
